title: crataegus azarolus subsp. triloba
intro: Crataegus azarolus subsp. triloba, also known as the Azarole hawthorn, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. The medicinal properties of Crataegus azarolus subsp. triloba include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. The herbal preparations of Crataegus azarolus subsp. triloba include tea, extracts, and capsules, which can be used to support cardiovascular health and provide antioxidant benefits.
